preview_text:
The model rates Ecuador as a heavy favourite, assigning them an 82% win probability. Curaçao's chances are minimal at just 1%, with a draw accounting for 16% of likely outcomes. This represents a decisive gap favouring the South American side, though the model retains meaningful probability mass on a stalemate.

The win–loss spread reflects considerable competitive separation. Ecuador's 82-point advantage over Curaçao's 1% is stark; a draw at 16% is the only plausible non-Ecuador outcome in the model's view. The match is unlikely to be close in expectation, though draws carry enough baseline probability that a 1–1 result would not shock. Ecuador are heavily positioned to control proceedings.

The sportsbook consensus and Polymarket prices both favour Ecuador more strongly than the model does, assigning them 87–88% win probability. They also attach less weight to draws (8–9%) and give Curaçao a higher floor at 4%. The gaps are 5 percentage points or less across outcomes, suggesting broad alignment in direction, though the books and crowd are modestly more confident in an Ecuador victory than the model.
